details.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2458-2466` only retries when `is_timeout_related` or `is_transient_codex_failure` returns true, but `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:39-50` does not recognize `AppError::InvocationTimeout`. `AgentExecutionService` emits `InvocationTimeout` for both adapter-promoted timeouts and the service safety-net timeout (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:259-264`, `395-410`), so reviewer and arbiter timeouts still fail immediately instead of using the transient retry loop.

The vote phase is still single-shot. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:986-1013` calls `invoke_final_review_member_once(...)` directly for `final_review:voter`, so a transient transport failure while collecting votes is still recorded as an immediate failure. On a minimum-quorum panel, one network blip during voting can still fail the whole final_review stage, which leaves the bead's reviewer-retry goal incomplete.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:154-163` hardcodes a fresh `RetryPolicy::default_policy()` for the new inner final-review retry loop instead of going through the existing test-stub backoff override path in `src/cli/run.rs:12549-12555`. That means any run started with `RALPH_BURNING_TEST_FAIL_INVOKE_STAGE=final_review` now pays the real 5/10/20/40s retry sleeps inside each reviewer/arbiter invocation before the stage fails. Existing failure-path scenarios such as `src/contexts/conformance_spec/scenarios.rs:4887-4891`, `12411-12415`, and `13474-13478` rely on that env seam to fail fast; with this branch they become minute-long retries instead of immediate deterministic failures.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2437-2445 makes every `FailureClass::DomainValidationFailure` terminal before the new transient classifier is consulted. That breaks the exact codex path this bead was meant to fix: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:2679-2705` explicitly treats `ERROR: stream disconnected before completion` / `You can retry your request...` carried as `DomainValidationFailure` as transient, but the final-review loop will still fail them immediately instead of retrying. The new no-retry test at src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:4494-4587 also hard-codes the wrong rule by asserting that all domain-validation failures are non-retryable, so a 1-reviewer panel can still fail on the reproduced codex disconnect path.

`fix-now` High: exhausted per-member retries still bubble up as retryable stage failures. In [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:172) and [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2390), `rewrite_transient_retry_exhaustion_error` preserves the original `FailureClass`, and `invoke_final_review_member` returns that retryable `TransportFailure`/`Timeout` after the member has already exhausted its own retry budget. The engine then sees that class in [src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5844) and retries the entire `final_review` stage again. That turns a supposed per-invocation retry into compounded stage-level retries, multiplying backend calls and backoff delays. The changed resume test at [tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:1227) now takes about 72s for exactly this reason. This violates the bead’s scope guard (`per-invocation transient retry, not per-stage restart`) and means an exhausted reviewer/arbiter failure is not actually terminal for the stage.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2457-2463` only treats `InvocationTimeout` and `InvocationFailed` as transient. `AgentExecutionService::invoke` always runs `check_availability` before the actual invocation (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:197-208`), and OpenRouter turns transient probe/network failures into `BackendUnavailable` (`src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:187-206`). Because `BackendUnavailable` is never considered retryable here, a temporary availability-probe blip on a final-review reviewer or arbiter still fails or degrades the panel immediately instead of consuming the new per-member retry budget.

Timeout retries in final review do not honor the existing retry policy. `invoke_final_review_member` hard-codes `retry_policy.max_attempts(FailureClass::TransportFailure)` at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2424-2426`, but the configured policy gives `FailureClass::Timeout` only 3 attempts at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:54-64`. As written, persistent reviewer/arbiter timeouts will consume 5 full timeout windows before failing instead of the intended 3.

High: final_review still bypasses the new per-member retry path during engine-side reviewer probing. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9487-9512, an optional reviewer that hits a transient check_availability error is dropped from the panel immediately, before execute_final_review_panel/invoke_final_review_member can apply the new retry loop. If that transient drop takes the panel below quorum, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9524-9544 returns AppError::InsufficientPanelMembers, but that error is non-retryable (src/shared/error.rs:345-354; src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5842-5863), so a single transient optional-reviewer probe failure can still hard-fail final_review. Even when quorum survives, the stage now votes with a smaller panel than configured, which can change consensus from a temporary backend blip. This needs to use the same transient classification/retry path as actual reviewer invocations instead of treating transient probe failures as durable panel shrinkage.

Medium: resume still treats transient final_review backend probe failures as stage drift. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2250-2287, resume re-checks the arbiter and required reviewers with adapter.check_availability and converts any non-exhaustion failure into ResumeDriftFailure. That means a temporary network/probe blip during `run resume` prevents the failed final_review stage from resuming at all, even though the invocation path in src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs would now retry the same backend once execution starts. Optional reviewers are also silently dropped on resume by src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2265-2299 for non-exhaustion probe errors, so resume can change panel membership for transient reasons.

Transient final-review availability failures still hard-fail `run start` and `run resume` before the new retry wrapper is ever reached. `execute_run` and `resume_run_with_retry_internal` still call `preflight_check` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1559`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2346-2356`), and the final-review branch there performs single-shot `check_availability` probes through `preflight_required_panel_target` / `preflight_panel_members` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:377-401`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:473-503`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:509-603`). A one-off arbiter/reviewer network blip in those preflights still aborts the whole run, so the original “single transient final_review backend error kills the run” failure mode remains reachable outside the stage-dispatch path.
